TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract sealed beforefieldinit ArmDomainRegistrationModelFactory extends System.Object

Docs.summary: A factory class for creating instances of the models for mocking.

static member AppServiceDomainData : Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier * string * Azure.Core.ResourceType * Azure.ResourceManager.Models.SystemData * System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary<string, string> * Azure.Core.AzureLocation * Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.RegistrationContactInfo * Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.RegistrationContactInfo * Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.RegistrationContactInfo * Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.RegistrationContactInfo * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDomainStatus> * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDomainProvisioningState> * seq<string> * Nullable<bool> * Nullable<DateTimeOffset> * Nullable<DateTimeOffset> * Nullable<DateTimeOffset> * Nullable<bool> * Nullable<bool> * seq<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostName> * Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainPurchaseConsent * seq<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainNotRenewableReason> * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDnsType> * string * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDnsType> * string * string -> Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.AppServiceDomainData
MemberSignature.Usage: Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.ArmDomainRegistrationModelFactory.AppServiceDomainData (id, name, resourceType, systemData, tags, location, contactAdmin, contactBilling, contactRegistrant, contactTech, registrationStatus, provisioningState, nameServers, isDomainPrivacyEnabled, createdOn, expireOn, lastRenewedOn, isAutoRenew, isDnsRecordManagementReady, managedHostNames, consent, domainNotRenewableReasons, dnsType, dnsZoneId, targetDnsType, authCode, kind)

Docs.param: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}.
The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts".
Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.
The geo-location where the resource lives.
true if domain privacy is enabled for this domain; otherwise, false.
Timestamp when the domain was renewed last time.
true if the domain should be automatically renewed; otherwise, false.

true if Azure can assign this domain to App Service apps; otherwise, false. This value will be true if domain registration status is active and \n it is hosted on name servers Azure has programmatic access to.
All hostnames derived from the domain and assigned to Azure resources.
Reasons why domain is not renewable.
Target DNS type (would be used for migration).

Docs.param: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}.
The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts".
Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.
true if domain privacy is enabled for this domain; otherwise, false.
Timestamp when the domain was renewed last time.
true if the domain should be automatically renewed; otherwise, false.

true if Azure can assign this domain to App Service apps; otherwise, false. This value will be true if domain registration status is active and \n it is hosted on name servers Azure has programmatic access to.
All hostnames derived from the domain and assigned to Azure resources.
Reasons why domain is not renewable.
Target DNS type (would be used for migration).

MemberSignature.Value: public static Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostName AppServiceHostName (string name = default, System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<string> siteNames = default, string azureResourceName = default, Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceResourceType? azureResourceType = default, Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.CustomHostNameDnsRecordType? customHostNameDnsRecordType = default, Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostNameType? hostNameType = default);

.method public static hidebysig class Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostName AppServiceHostName(string name, class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<string> siteNames, string azureResourceName, valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceResourceType> azureResourceType, valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.CustomHostNameDnsRecordType> customHostNameDnsRecordType, valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostNameType> hostNameType) cil managed

Public Shared Function AppServiceHostName (Optional name As String = Nothing, Optional siteNames As IEnumerable(Of String) = Nothing, Optional azureResourceName As String = Nothing, Optional azureResourceType As Nullable(Of AppServiceResourceType) = Nothing, Optional customHostNameDnsRecordType As Nullable(Of CustomHostNameDnsRecordType) = Nothing, Optional hostNameType As Nullable(Of AppServiceHostNameType) = Nothing) As AppServiceHostName

static member AppServiceHostName : string * seq<string> * string * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceResourceType> * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.CustomHostNameDnsRecordType> * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostNameType> -> Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceHostName

Docs.param: List of apps the hostname is assigned to. This list will have more than one app only if the hostname is pointing to a Traffic Manager.
Name of the Azure resource the hostname is assigned to. If it is assigned to a Traffic Manager then it will be the Traffic Manager name otherwise it will be the app name.
Type of the Azure resource the hostname is assigned to.
Docs.summary: Details of a hostname derived from a domain.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainAvailabilityCheckResult DomainAvailabilityCheckResult (string name = default, bool? isAvailable = default, Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDomainType? domainType = default);

.method public static hidebysig class Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainAvailabilityCheckResult DomainAvailabilityCheckResult(string name, valuetype System.Nullable`1<bool> isAvailable, valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDomainType> domainType) cil managed

Public Shared Function DomainAvailabilityCheckResult (Optional name As String = Nothing, Optional isAvailable As Nullable(Of Boolean) = Nothing, Optional domainType As Nullable(Of AppServiceDomainType) = Nothing) As DomainAvailabilityCheckResult

static member DomainAvailabilityCheckResult : string * Nullable<bool> * Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.AppServiceDomainType> -> Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainAvailabilityCheckResult

Docs.param: true if domain can be purchased using CreateDomain API; otherwise, false.
Valid values are Regular domain: Azure will charge the full price of domain registration, SoftDeleted: Purchasing this domain will simply restore it and this operation will not cost anything.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainControlCenterSsoRequestInfo DomainControlCenterSsoRequestInfo (string uri = default, string postParameterKey = default, string postParameterValue = default);

static member DomainControlCenterSsoRequestInfo : string * string * string -> Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainControlCenterSsoRequestInfo

Docs.param: URL where the single sign-on request is to be made.
Post parameter value. Client should use 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' encoding for this value.
Docs.summary: Single sign-on request information for domain management.

The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts".
Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

.method public static hidebysig class Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainPurchaseConsent DomainPurchaseConsent(class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<string> agreementKeys, string agreedBy, valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype System.DateTimeOffset> agreedOn) cil managed

Public Shared Function DomainPurchaseConsent (Optional agreementKeys As IEnumerable(Of String) = Nothing, Optional agreedBy As String = Nothing, Optional agreedOn As Nullable(Of DateTimeOffset) = Nothing) As DomainPurchaseConsent
static member DomainPurchaseConsent : seq<string> * string * Nullable<DateTimeOffset> -> Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.Models.DomainPurchaseConsent

Docs.param: List of applicable legal agreement keys. This list can be retrieved using ListLegalAgreements API under TopLevelDomain resource.
Timestamp when the agreements were accepted.
Docs.summary: Domain purchase consent object, representing acceptance of applicable legal agreements.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Azure.ResourceManager.DomainRegistration.TopLevelDomainData TopLevelDomainData (Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier id = default, string name = default, Azure.Core.ResourceType resourceType = default, Azure.ResourceManager.Models.SystemData systemData = default, bool? isDomainPrivacySupported = default, string kind = default);

Docs.param: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}.
The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts".
Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.
If true, then the top level domain supports domain privacy; otherwise, false.

Docs.summary: Legal agreement for a top level domain.
